title: ExoVeil: How to Find a Planet by a Single Shadow
oneliner: A new algorithm detects single transits of planets that were previously invisible.
description: Usually you need to see a planet pass in front of its star multiple times, but ExoVeil can do it in one go — like spotting a fish by a single ripple.

title: How to Catch an Invisible Particle: Neutrinos in Pictures
oneliner: Scientists turn messy neutrino telescope data into simple color images so a neural network can easily tell where the particle came from.
description: Researchers from the IceCube project in Antarctica developed a method that converts neutrino signals into RGB images of 72×72 pixels. A neural network uses these pictures to determine the particle's direction almost as accurately as complex models.

title: Cosmic Vermicelli: A Carbon Chain in a Protoplanetary Disk
oneliner: Astronomers have for the first time found a long carbon molecule in a disk where planets are forming—it's like discovering a complex ingredient in a cosmic soup that's still being cooked.
description: Using the ALMA radio telescope, scientists detected the molecule HC5N—one of the longest carbon chains—in the disk around a young star. This shows that complex organic chemistry is possible even in cold regions where planets are born.

title: Nereid: The Last Native Moon of Neptune
oneliner: New data shows that Nereid is not a captured asteroid but a surviving family member.
description: Imagine a harbor where ships peacefully rested—these were the regular [tag:moon]moons[/tag] around [tag:planet]Neptune[/tag]. Suddenly a huge pirate ship bursts in—the captured Triton. Its [tag:gravity]gravity[/tag] creates chaos: some moons are shattered, others flung into space. Only one—Nereid—su

title: How to Synchronize Telescopes with Femtosecond Precision
oneliner: A new combiner panel synchronizes hundreds of telescopes with femtosecond precision.
description: A panel has been developed to combine signals from 128 telescopes. It enables the study of dark matter and the early universe.

title: Three Ways to Weigh a Neutron Star, and They All Differ
oneliner: Astronomers are trying to determine the mass of a neutron star paired with a white dwarf, but different methods yield different answers.
description: One of the densest stars in the universe refuses to let scientists pin down its precise characteristics.

title: Is the Universe Slightly Twisting Light? A Discovery on the Edge
oneliner: Scientists found a way to distinguish real polarization rotation of light from telescope errors and spotted a faint hint of new physics.
description: Studying ancient light from the Big Bang, researchers found that its oscillations might be slightly rotated—as if space itself were 'twisting' the rays.

title: AI Seismologist Protects Gravitational Wave Search
oneliner: Neural network SeismoGPT predicts earthquakes to help gravitational wave detectors work without interference.
description: SeismoGPT is an artificial intelligence that learns to anticipate seismic vibrations to shield gravitational wave observatories from Earth's noise.

title: The Culinary Secret of Neutron Stars
oneliner: Neutron stars cool slower thanks to thermonuclear cooking in their crust.
description: Astrophysicists have uncovered a secret: neutron stars remain hot after flares because thermonuclear fusion continues in their crust.
